explanation: The property states that globally, whenever the autopilot is in the 'TRANSITION' state, it must be the case that the conditions 'supported' and 'good' hold continuously until the 'NOMINAL' state is eventually reached. This accurately captures the requirement that the transition from 'TRANSITION' to 'NOMINAL' must occur only while the system is supported and sensor data is good, and that the 'supported' and 'good' conditions must be maintained throughout this transition period.

This LTL formula states that globally (G), if the system is currently in the TRANSITION state (`state_is_TRANSITION`) AND both the system is supported (`supported`) AND the sensor data is good (`good`) at that moment, THEN in the very next state (X), the system must be in the NOMINAL state (`state_is_NOMINAL`). This precisely captures the requirement that the autopilot changes from TRANSITION to NOMINAL immediately when the specified conditions are met, implying a direct state transition.

The LTL property "G (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good -> X state_is_NOMINAL)" captures the requirement by stating that globally (G), if the autopilot is currently in the 'TRANSITION' state AND the system is 'supported' AND the sensor data is 'good', then in the very next state (X), the autopilot must be in the 'NOMINAL' state. This accurately represents the command for an immediate state change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL upon the conjunction of the specified conditions.

The property states that globally, if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state AND the system is supported AND sensor data is good, then the autopilot must remain in the TRANSITION state UNTIL it enters the NOMINAL state, and the NOMINAL state must eventually be reached. This accurately captures the conditional state change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL upon the satisfaction of both 'supported' and 'good' conditions. The 'until' operator ensures that the system stays in the origin state until the destination state is reached, which is inherent in the phrase 'change states from A to B'.

The property states that globally (G), if the autopilot is currently in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION), is not yet in the NOMINAL state (!state_is_NOMINAL), and the conditions 'system is supported' (supported) and 'sensor data is good' (good) are simultaneously true, then eventually (F) the autopilot must transition to the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL) and no longer be in the TRANSITION state (!state_is_TRANSITION). This formulation accurately captures the specified state change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL only when the defined conditions are met.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the autopilot is currently in the TRANSITION state AND the system is supported AND sensor data is good, then in the very next state (X), the autopilot must be in the NOMINAL state. This directly captures the requirement that the state change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL must occur immediately when the specified conditions are met.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION) AND the system is supported (supported) AND the sensor data is good (good), THEN it must eventually (F) change to the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL). This captures the requirement that the transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL is conditional upon both 'supported' and 'good' being true at the time the system is in the TRANSITION state. The 'F' operator allows for the transition to occur after some time, rather than instantaneously in the next state.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the autopilot is currently in the 'TRANSITION' state (`state_is_TRANSITION`) AND the system is supported (`supported`) AND the sensor data is good (`good`), THEN in the very next time step (X), the autopilot MUST be in the 'NOMINAL' state (`state_is_NOMINAL`). This directly captures the requirement that the autopilot changes state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL as an immediate consequence when the specified conditions are met. The 'when' clause is interpreted as a trigger for an immediate state change.

This LTL formula states that globally (G), if the system is currently in the TRANSITION state (`state_is_TRANSITION`) AND the system is supported (`supported`) AND sensor data is good (`good`), THEN in the very next state (X), the system must be in the NOMINAL state (`state_is_NOMINAL`). This captures the immediate and conditional state transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL as specified by the 'when' clause.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the autopilot is currently in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION) AND the system is supported (supported) AND the sensor data is good (good), then eventually (F) the autopilot will change to the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL). This captures the requirement for the state transition to NOMINAL to occur when the specified conditions are met while in the TRANSITION state.
